_description: Complete transaction type business logic — what each type DOES in the CertifyOS system
what_is_transaction_type: pdmTransactionType defines what action the system performs on a roster record. It determines: required fields, database operations, and whether entities are created/updated/terminated.

Add to Network.description: Provider joining a network for the first time or being added to a new network
Add to Network.what_happens: Links practitioner to network within their group, sets network effective date

Add to Plan.description: Adding provider to a health plan within their network
Add to Plan.what_happens: Associates provider with a plan, sets plan effective date

Add to Group.description: Provider joining a new practice group
Add to Group.what_happens: Links practitioner to group with effective date

Add Role.description: Adding a new role (PCP/Specialist) to existing provider

Add Specialty.description: Adding a new specialty to existing provider

Add Location.description: Adding provider to a new practice location
Add Location.what_happens: Associates practitioner with a physical address, sets location type PRI/PRA

Update Information.description: Updating existing provider data (name, address, credentials, etc.)
Update Information.what_happens: Only changed fields are updated, no new relationships created

Terminate from Network.description: Provider LEAVING an entire network (all locations within that network are terminated)
Terminate from Network.database_action: UPSERT_DATA on TenantGroupPractitionerNetwork with terminationDate, cascades to TenantGroupLocationPractitionerNetwork
Terminate from Network.what_happens: 1) Resolves NPI → certifyId → tenantPractitionerId. 2) Fetches all relationships. 3) Finds networkId. 4) Sets terminationDate on network record. 5) Cascades to all locations within that network.
Terminate from Network.cascade: Terminates ALL locations under this network for this provider

Terminate from Location.description: Provider LEAVING one specific location (other locations stay active)
Terminate from Location.what_happens: 1) Resolves NPI → practitioner IDs. 2) Finds specific location. 3) Sets terminationDate on location record only. 4) Removes location-network-specialty relationships.
Terminate from Location.cascade: Only terminates the specific location, NOT the network

Terminate from Group.description: Provider LEAVING an entire group (all networks, locations, specialties under that group are terminated)
Terminate from Group.what_happens: Terminates entire group relationship. Cascades to terminate ALL child relationships.
Terminate from Group.cascade: Terminates ALL networks, ALL locations, ALL specialties under this group

termination_hierarchy.description: Terminations cascade DOWN the hierarchy. Terminate from Group affects everything below it.
termination_hierarchy.terminate_from_group: Terminates: group + all networks + all locations + all specialties + all plans
termination_hierarchy.terminate_from_network: Terminates: network + all locations under that network + specialties + plans
termination_hierarchy.terminate_from_location: Terminates: location + specialties at that location only

processing_flow.step_2: ActionTypeValidationService validates required fields for this transaction type
processing_flow.step_3_termination: If termination: call terminatePractitionerFromHandler() with type=NETWORK/GROUP_LOCATIONS/GROUP
processing_flow.step_3_normal: If non-termination: TransactionBuilderService.buildTransaction() maps CSV to entity operations
processing_flow.step_4_termination: Fetch existing relationships, set terminationDate via UPSERT_DATA batch operations
processing_flow.step_4_normal: Execute via Pub/Sub (async) or synchronous transactional batch
processing_flow.step_5: Update roster record status: COMPLETED or FAILED

termination_date_field_standardization.description: All termination date fields are standardized to 'terminationDate' internally

database_operations.FIND_OR_CREATE: Find by key, create if not exists
database_operations.UPSERT_DATA: Insert or update specific fields (used for terminations)

key_insight_for_ai: When the AI sees a roster file, it must understand: 1) Non-term rows create NEW relationships in the system. 2) Term rows UPDATE existing relationships with termination dates. 3) Required fields differ per transaction type. 4) The effective date field CHANGES MEANING based on transaction type (network effective vs termination date). 5) Location Type (PRI/PRA) matters for Add operations.
